title: Polynomial Representation and Operations
description: A visual guide showing two primary methods for representing polynomials (Array and Linked List) and their applications in fundamental operations.
relationships: Array is efficient for dense polynomials with sequential exponents
Linked List is efficient for sparse polynomials with non-sequential exponents
Addition traverses both structures adding coefficients of like terms
Multiplication creates intermediate products by multiplying each term of first polynomial with every term of the second
Evaluation efficiently computes the polynomial's value for a given variable input
